_doc: Implementation backlog for the single sequential Ralph loop. This is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for done/not-done and dependency order; docs/HARNESS.md describes how the loop reads it (§8.1 = schema). These tasks are ILLUSTRATIVE EXAMPLES showing the schema, the gate markers, and per-task model selection — REPLACE them with your own atomic, dependency-ordered tasks before running the loop.

tasks.title: Project scaffold + CI green on an empty build
tasks.do: Lay down the minimal project skeleton for your stack and wire the real format/lint/test/build commands into CI so an empty build goes green. Mechanical boilerplate — runs on Sonnet, escalates to Opus if it can't get CI green.
tasks.doneWhen: README.md documents how to build/run; CI is green on main; the Definition of Done commands in docs/HARNESS.md §5 match what CI runs.

tasks.title: Core module skeleton + public interface
tasks.do: Define the central module's public interface (types/traits/functions) that later tasks build against, with stub implementations and unit tests for the contract.
tasks.doneWhen: the interface compiles, is unit-tested, and is documented; downstream tasks can code against it without touching its internals.

tasks.title: First feature against the core interface
tasks.do: Implement the first real feature using the T002 interface.
tasks.doneWhen: feature works, unit + integration tests pass, README status row flipped.

tasks.title: Integration test harness
tasks.do: Build an end-to-end test that exercises the feature against a realistic input, and run the app once against real input to confirm it behaves.
tasks.doneWhen: the e2e test passes and the empirical run is recorded in worklog/T004.md. A human reviews this deliverable before dependent tasks proceed.

tasks.title: Provision external resource
tasks.do: Prepare everything for the external resource (config, scripts, docs) up to the point a human must run the privileged/credentialed step.
tasks.doneWhen: everything around the manual step is ready and documented; the agent records failed:blocked (needs-human) — the human performs the one-time provisioning step.
